course_title: Complex Problem Solving in Health Care Management and Policy
subject: HPM
course_description: This class introduces essential techniques and strategies to effectively address unstructured, complex problems that managers and health policy analysts often encounter during their careers. Throughout the course, students will learn the key steps in problem-solving for unstructured problems; including defining the problem, disaggregating the problem into solvable questions using common tools like issue trees, identifying data needs and appropriate analytic approaches, choosing evaluation metrics, creating a workplans, identifying solutions and how to sell them. The course will conclude with a problem-solving competition among student groups applying the skills learn to solve a complex healthcare problem.
